title: Peppery Crispy Algerian Yautia Bowl
description: A spicy, crispy bowl featuring spherified yautia with a peppery spice blend, finished on an iron teppan for a crunchy finish.
instructions: Preheat an iron teppan over medium-high heat until hot.
Peel, grate, and squeeze out excess moisture from the yautia tuber, collecting 300g of puree.
In a mixing bowl, combine the yautia puree with salt, cumin, coriander, paprika, black pepper, lemon zest, and parsley.
Dissolve the sodium alginate in 100ml of water to form a smooth solution, then whisk it into the yautia mixture until fully blended.
Prepare a calcium bath by dissolving calcium lactate in the remaining 200ml of water.
Using a spoon or syringe, drop small spoonfuls of the yautia mixture into the calcium bath; let them sit for two minutes before transferring to cold water to halt the reaction.
Drain the spheres and pat them dry with a paper towel, then lightly brush each sphere with olive oil.
Place the spheres on the preheated teppan; sear each side for about one minute until they develop a crisp golden crust.
Arrange the crispy spheres in a bowl, drizzle with any remaining olive oil, and scatter chopped parsley over the top.
Serve immediately, optionally squeezing a little fresh lemon juice over the bowl for extra brightness.
